finding_results.notes: Two edits applied. (1) Line 71: Dashboard emotional triggers entry changed from 'He died when she was nineteen' to 'He moved to Seattle when she was twelve — told her it was temporary, made it permanent. He used to tell her she was the most interesting person in any room, and then he left. This is her deepest vulnerability — someone who makes her feel seen the way he did before he chose to stop.' No death language remains; move to Seattle is mentioned; emotional trigger logic (someone who makes her feel seen) preserved; age reference consistent with leaving at 12. (2) Line 203: 'her father's funeral' changed to 'the night her father left for Seattle' — removes the funeral reference (no funeral if the father is alive) while preserving the emotional magnitude of the crying passage. Grep confirms no remaining death/funeral/dying references to the father in the chapter (remaining 'died'/'dead' hits are about Nora's phone).

Quality audit completed. Chapter audited for constitution compliance: heading contract preserved (line 1: '# Chapter 15'); no elliptical clipping in narration or dialogue; dialogue register matches high-contraction profiles for both Nora and Simone (verified all dialogue lines use natural contractions); no over-formal dialogue detected; no transcript cadence in dialogue exchanges (leverage shifts present in the dinner scene where Simone asks about Caleb and Nora performs normalcy); no sanitization — the intimate photo, financial tracking, and emotional devastation are rendered with full specificity; prose style matches close-third Nora POV with sensory bias toward touch, sound, and spatial pressure; no style bible example lines appear verbatim. The CRIT-04 continuity fix at lines 71 and 203 constitutes the grounded fix required by the acceptance test.

pass_summaries.summary: Two findings addressed, both FIXED. The critical continuity error (CRIT-04) required edits at two locations: the OpenClaw dashboard's emotional triggers entry (line 71) and Nora's crying passage (line 203). Both references to the father dying have been replaced with the canonical version — the father moved to Seattle when Nora was twelve, alive but absent. The emotional trigger logic ('someone who makes her feel seen') is preserved. The quality audit (fallback_chapter_15_contract) confirmed the chapter is constitution-compliant: natural dialogue register, no clipping, no sanitization, no formality drift, heading contract intact. No additional craft defects requiring revision were identified.

summary: Merged three-pass revision report (FIXED=2, PARTIAL=0, UNRESOLVED=0).
